cards.front: What is the difference between an ordinary variable and an environment variable in shell programming?
cards.back: An ordinary variable is a user-defined variable that is local to a shell script, while an environment variable is a variable that is set by the shell or the operating system.

cards.front: How to display the value of an environment variable in a shell script?
cards.back: You can display the value of an environment variable in a shell script using the $variable syntax, where variable is the name of the environment variable.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the export command in shell programming?
cards.back: The export command is used to set an environment variable and make it available to all child processes created by the shell.

cards.front: How to remove a variable from the environment in a shell script?
cards.back: You can remove a variable from the environment in a shell script using the unset command, followed by the name of the variable, e.g., unset variable_name.

cards.front: What is the difference between the export command and the declare command in shell programming?
cards.back: The export command is used to set a variable as an environment variable, while the declare command is used to set a variable as a local variable to the shell, regardless of whether it's a shell variable or an environment variable.
